It saddens me to share the news that my father Bill Schweikert died this morning

He was a good man, a great father, and a wonderful grandfather to our children Olivia and Matthew.

I will always cherish this picture of him with Matthew that was taken last night just hours before https://t.co/GR0xzAFdvY

Medicaid spending is already up 9.7% this year and could finish in double digits while we borrow about $90,000 a second.

At some point, we have to lower the cost of delivering care instead of asking taxpayers to pay more without people getting healthier. https://t.co/M0oJRprwvp
Thank you to our district office interns for all of your hard work serving our office and the people of AZ01! 🇺🇸🌵 https://t.co/yvvsAZk3TN

America spends about $25 BILLION a week just on interest. 

25 cents of every tax dollar goes to interest, now the second-largest expense in the federal budget, ahead of Medicare and national defense. 

Keep borrowing like this and the bond market runs the country.

This is exactly why I introduced the Combating Deceptive Practices in Assistance Programs Act. 

States should have to prove someone cannot perform at least three basic daily activities before taxpayers pay for Medicaid home care.

Congratulations to Roshni Nagarankanti and Merissa Richey on earning the Congressional Award. 

More than 400 hours of volunteer service reflects an extraordinary commitment to others. I’m proud to recognize these outstanding young leaders from our district. https://t.co/XNNMmMI7Dn

Our kids inherit that debt while crime and corruption drain federal programs. 

Reconciliation should lower spending, not keep taxpayers paying for broken government. https://t.co/3mVl9D8XDx

In New York, one program grew from $1 billion to $11 billion in a few years. Home health aides per capita are about three times the national average.

Waste, fraud and abuse could make up a quarter of the state’s Medicaid spending. https://t.co/pcngS8SmSm

Phone carriers and internet providers must use the technology they have to block these scams before more families lose their life savings.

ACA broker spending jumped from $1.9B in 2020 to a projected $5.5B in 2025.

Are families getting better help choosing health plans, or are taxpayers funding broker commissions that drive up federal spending? https://t.co/VdZI1QdHJ8
RT @JECRepublicans: https://t.co/Z5q1izp8pw

$6.5 billion in fake health care claims.

Great work catching it. Now use the data so Medicare and Medicaid stop more of these payments before taxpayers get robbed.

Every 13.5 seconds, Washington borrows another $1 million.

The data says my three-year-old would need every dime he will ever make to pay for promises the federal government has already made.

Welcome to the morality of the modern Congress. https://t.co/mBuCyxjA6S

I joined @SecDuffy and the I-11 Coalition to discuss Arizona’s interstate priorities.

I-11 is a major project for Arizona, and we’re working to move it up the federal priority list while bringing better technology to the permitting process. https://t.co/mVdPDg3zo9

I hope today brings you the gratitude, laughter and time with family you deserve. https://t.co/r9Hs2vjiBs
